children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This subpart may be cited as the “Gun-Free Schools Act”.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State receiving Federal funds under any subchapter of this chapter shall have in effect a State law requiring local educational agencies to expel from school for a period of not less than 1 year a student who is determined to have brought a firearm to a school, or to have possessed a firearm at a school, under the jurisdiction of local educational agencies in that State, except that such State law shall allow the chief administering officer of a local educational agency to modify such expulsion requirement for a student on a case-by-case basis if such modification is in writing.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 2 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subpart shall be construed to prevent a State from allowing a local educational agency that has expelled a student from such a student’s regular school setting from providing educational services to such student in an alternative setting.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 3 Definition
children.text: For the purpose of this section, the term “firearm” has the same meaning given such term in .section 921(a) of title 18

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: c Special rule
children.text: The provisions of this section shall be construed in a manner consistent with the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 1400
an assurance that such local educational agency is in compliance with the State law required by subsection (b); and

children.label: A
children.text: the name of the school concerned;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of students expelled from such school; and

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: e Reporting
children.text: Each State shall report the information described in subsection (d) to the Secretary on an annual basis.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: f Definition
children.text: For the purpose of subsection (d), the term “school” means any setting that is under the control and supervision of the local educational agency for the purpose of student activities approved and authorized by the local educational agency.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: g Exception
children.text: Nothing in this section shall apply to a firearm that is lawfully stored inside a locked vehicle on school property, or if it is for activities approved and authorized by the local educational agency and the local educational agency adopts appropriate safeguards to ensure student safety.

children.heading: Policy regarding criminal justice system referral
children.label: h Policy regarding criminal justice system referral

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No funds shall be made available under any subchapter of this chapter to any local educational agency unless such agency has a policy requiring referral to the criminal justice or juvenile delinquency system of any student who brings a firearm or weapon to a school served by such agency.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 2 Definition
children.text: For the purpose of this subsection, the term “school” has the same meaning given to such term by .section 921(a) of title 18
